meta.title: Spacer
meta.description: A simple layout component that automatically fills available space in flex layouts using flexGrow 1.

mdx:
## Overview

Spacer is a utility component designed to create flexible spacing between
elements in flex layouts. It automatically expands to fill any remaining space,
making it perfect for creating navigation bars, toolbars, and other layouts
where you need to push elements apart.

### Key features

- **Automatic Space Filling**: Uses `flexGrow: 1` to occupy all available space
- **Works in Any Direction**: Supports both horizontal and vertical flex layouts
- **Flexible Styling**: Can accept any Chakra UI Box props for custom styling
- **Zero Configuration**: Works out of the box with no required props

## Variables

Get familiar with the features.

### Basic Usage

The most common use case is pushing elements to opposite ends of a container.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="row" width="full">
    <Button variant="solid">Left</Button>
    <Spacer />
    <Button variant="outline">Right</Button>
  </Stack>
)
```

### Navigation Layout

Create navigation-style layouts with multiple element groups.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="row" width="full">
    <Button variant="ghost">Home</Button>
    <Button variant="ghost">About</Button>
    <Button variant="ghost">Services</Button>
    <Spacer />
    <Button variant="ghost">Profile</Button>
    <Button variant="solid">Sign In</Button>
  </Stack>
)
```

### Multiple Spacers

Use multiple spacers to distribute space evenly between elements.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="row" width="full">
    <Button variant="outline">First</Button>
    <Spacer />
    <Button variant="outline">Second</Button>
    <Spacer />
    <Button variant="outline">Third</Button>
  </Stack>
)
```

### Vertical Layout

Spacer works in vertical layouts too.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="column" height="400px" width="200px" borderWidth="1px" borderRadius="200">
    <Box padding="400" backgroundColor="neutral.3" borderRadius="200 200 0 0">
      Header
    </Box>
    <Spacer />
    <Box padding="400" backgroundColor="neutral.3" borderRadius="0 0 200 200">
      Footer
    </Box>
  </Stack>
)
```

### With Custom Styling

Spacer accepts all Chakra UI Box props for custom styling.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="row" width="full">
    <Button variant="solid">Start</Button>
    <Spacer backgroundColor="primary.3" borderRadius="200" minHeight="40px" />
    <Button variant="solid">End</Button>
  </Stack>
)
```

overview.mdx:
## Overview

Spacer is a utility component designed to create flexible spacing between
elements in flex layouts. It automatically expands to fill any remaining space,
making it perfect for creating navigation bars, toolbars, and other layouts
where you need to push elements apart.

### Key features

- **Automatic Space Filling**: Uses `flexGrow: 1` to occupy all available space
- **Works in Any Direction**: Supports both horizontal and vertical flex layouts
- **Flexible Styling**: Can accept any Chakra UI Box props for custom styling
- **Zero Configuration**: Works out of the box with no required props

## Variables

Get familiar with the features.

### Basic Usage

The most common use case is pushing elements to opposite ends of a container.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="row" width="full">

    <Button variant="outline">Right</Button>

```

### Navigation Layout

Create navigation-style layouts with multiple element groups.

```jsx live
const App = () => (

    <Button variant="ghost">Profile</Button>

```

### Multiple Spacers

Use multiple spacers to distribute space evenly between elements.

```jsx live

  <Stack direction="row" width="full">

    <Button variant="outline">Second</Button>
```

### Vertical Layout

```jsx live
const App = () => (
```

a11y.mdx:
## Accessibility

Accessibility ensures that digital content and functionality are usable by
everyone, including people with disabilities, by addressing visual, auditory,
cognitive, and physical limitations.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="row" width="full">
    <Button variant="solid">Left</Button>
    <Spacer />
    <Button variant="outline">Right</Button>
  </Stack>
)
```

### Accessibility standards

- **Semantic Structure**: Spacer should not affect document semantics or screen
  reader navigation
- **Visual Only**: The spacing created by Spacer is purely visual and should not
  convey meaning
- **Alternative Approaches**: If spacing conveys important information, consider
  using semantic HTML elements with appropriate ARIA attributes

guidelines.mdx:
## Guidelines

Use this component strategically to create flexible layouts.

### Best practices

- **Use in Flex Containers**: Spacer only works within flex or grid layouts
  (Stack, HStack, VStack, etc.)
- **Semantic HTML**: Spacer renders a `<div>` element, so use it appropriately
  in your document structure
- **Multiple Spacers**: When using multiple spacers, they will divide the
  available space equally
- **Custom Styling**: You can pass any Box props to customize appearance when
  needed

### When to use

> [!TIP]\
> When to use

- **Navigation Bars**: Separate logo from menu items or push actions to the
  right
- **Toolbars**: Group related tools with flexible spacing between groups
- **Card Footers**: Push action buttons to opposite ends
- **Forms**: Create space between form sections or buttons
- **Headers**: Separate title from actions or utilities

> [!CAUTION]\
> When not to use

- **Fixed Spacing**: Use Stack's `gap` prop or Box's `margin` for consistent,
  fixed spacing
- **Non-Flex Layouts**: Spacer requires a flex or grid parent to work
- **Complex Grids**: Use CSS Grid for two-dimensional layouts instead
- **Content Spacing**: Don't use for spacing within text content - use
  typography components
